granite.heading: Granite is our specialty.
granite.lede: It's in the name. We design, fabricate, and install granite countertops for kitchens, bathrooms, and commercial spaces — featuring MSI granite slabs in every color the earth makes.

cta.text: Found your granite? See the full slab in Ardmore.

quartz.heading: Dazzling quartz, installed flawlessly.
quartz.lede: We recommend Q™ Premium Natural Quartz and also carry CaesarStone, Cambria, Silestone, and Zodiaq. Low-maintenance, non-porous, and consistent — designed, fabricated, and installed by one crew.

cta.text: Found your quartz? We'll pull a sample for your visit.

marble.heading: Marble from around the world.
marble.lede1: Carrara, Calacatta, and beyond — we carry top-quality marble slabs and design, fabricate, and install marble countertops in kitchens, bathrooms, and commercial spaces.
marble.lede2: Marble is a softer, honed stone that rewards a little care with a look nothing else matches. We'll walk you through where it shines — vanities, baking counters, fireplace surrounds — and where a quartz or granite might serve you better.
marble.alt: Calacatta marble countertop edge detail with gold veining

cta.text: Come run your hand over the Calacatta.

naturalStone.heading: Beyond granite and marble.
naturalStone.lede: Travertine, limestone, slate, and onyx each bring something granite can't. We'll help you pick the right stone for the right room — and steer you away from the wrong one.

stones.name: Travertine
stones.desc: An affordable, natural look harvested around mineral springs. Similar strength to marble with a warmly pitted texture — slabs are sealed at install and resealed on a schedule we'll set up for you.

stones.name: Limestone
stones.desc: Centuries of buildings can't be wrong: elegant, available, and affordable. Muted tones that read like marble at a friendlier price. As a calcium carbonate it dislikes acid, so we'll talk through where it belongs.

stones.name: Slate
stones.desc: The overlooked workhorse. Fine-grained, naturally resistant to fading, chemicals, and constant traffic — equally at home on a bathroom floor, an outdoor walkway, or a dramatic feature wall.

stones.name: Onyx
stones.desc: A precious, translucent stone — light can shine straight through it, which opens spectacular backlit possibilities. Softer than granite, so we reserve it for backsplashes, bath counters, and low-wear showpieces.

cta.text: Not sure which stone? That's what the showroom is for.
